content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 14, 1987

Michael Anderson
The Council for Political
Responsiveness
2261 Market Street, Ste. 225
San Francisco, CA 94114-1693

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-87-162

Dear Mr. Anderson:

You have requested advice concerning the disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

Is the Council for Political Responsiveness (CPR) required to
file disclosure reports under the provisions of the Political
Reform Act?

CONCLUSION

Based on the materials enclosed with your letter, it appears
that CPR merely provides information about and encourages
participation in the political process. If that is the case, CPR
has no filing obligations under the Act. However, your letter
provides no specific details about CPR's activities, and therefore
it is not possible to provide you with a definite conclusion. The
information contained in the analysis section of this letter
should help you in determining whether CPR is required to file
disclosure reports.

FACTS

According to the brochure enclosed with your letter, CPR is a
non-partisan, non-profit community organization whose purpose is:

...to promote and support the principle that each citizen
is equally responsible for voting on public issues,
assisting in the development of governmental policy and
maintaining the integrity of the persons in offices of
authority.

_________________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Michael Anderson
July 14, 1987
Page 2

The brochure also states that CPR's specific programs include:

•    Generating increased participation in public voting
     through media campaigns and special promotions.

•    Providing the public with effective, concise, factual
     information on significant governmental actions and
     policy issues.

•    Inception and continual augmentation of a public-servant-
     accountability-system that is easily accessible to the
     average person.

•    Recording and publishing a model-system-manual for
     start-up and operation of similar organizations in other
     communities.

•    Providing guidelines and activities for public access of
     the legislative body that most concerns them in order to
     help the public create positive change or input an
     effective voice in the process of policy-making.

•    Promoting increased awareness in the significance of each
     person's vote and each person's correspondence to
     legislators on issues.

     In addition to the brochure, you provided a "CPR Infosheet" of
publications which are available to interested persons from state
and federal government agencies as well as other politically
active organizations.

                              ANALYSIS

     As stated previously, your letter does not provide any
specific details about CPR's activities and how it accomplishes
the purposes.  Therefore, I hope the following general information
will help you to determine whether CPR's activities will create
disclosure obligations under the Act.

     The Act requires a membership organization like CPR to file
campaign disclosure reports if it receives $500 in campaign
contributions during a calendar year.  Membership dues are
"contributions" to CPR if CPR uses them for the purpose of making
contributions to or expenditures on behalf of state or local
officeholders, candidates or committees.  (Sections 82013(a),
84100-84217.)  Enclosed is a copy of the 1987 "Information Manual
on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act"
which you should use to determine CPR's filing obligations if $500
in a calendar year is spent to support or oppose officeholders,
candidates or committees.

Michael Anderson
July 14, 1987
Page 3

In addition, organizations which employ a lobbyist or a
lobbying firm for the purpose of attempting to influence state
legislative or administrative actions incur filing obligations
under the lobbying disclosure provisions of the Act. (Sections
86100-86300.) Organizations which do not employ a lobbyist or a
lobbying firm, but which spend $5,000 in a calendar quarter (other
than campaign contributions) to influence state legislative or
administrative actions also must file lobbying disclosure reports.
(Section 86115.) Enclosed is a copy of the 1987 "Information
Manual on Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform
Act" which you should use to determine CPR's filing obligations if
money is being spent to influence legislative or administrative
actions.

I hope the foregoing has been helpful. If you need further
assistance with regard to CPR's possible filing obligations,
please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Carla J. Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant

DMG:CJW:kmt

Enclosures

89-162
LUKE-DUDI
Civil Engineers, Inc.

THE COUNCIL FOR POLITICAL
RESPONSIVENESS
2261 Market Street
Suite 225
San Francisco, CA 94114-1693

Greg Luke, P.E.

Water & Wastewater Facilities • Waste
Runoff Control • Water Quality Studies •
Pavement Design • Computer S
Construction Inspection

605 Third Street Encinitas, CA 92024

A 87-162

June 12, 1987

Director
Executive/Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA     95814

Gentlemen:

We are writing to request an official Letter of Determinatio
indicating whether our organization; CPR/qualifies as a
"Political Committee" under your terms.

We would like to maintain a close and positive relationship
with the FPPC in the future and wanted to confirm that our
filings were in order.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Michael Anderson
Administrative Associate
CPR

Review Materials Enclosed

MA/bb

3/87
Publications:
Some of the following publications are available for free and some have
a small mailing or printing fee. Write or call the publisher for
details. Some of the publications may exhibit a certain bias. Please
read objectively. CPR does not endorse any publication as a final source
of information.

CPR INFOSHEET                                                           1

1. THE FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT. WHAT IT MEANS TO YOU./ CPR
Publications, 2261 Market Street, SF, CA 94114-1693

2. THE SECRECY LOBBY, SAN FRANCISCO BAY GUARDIAN, VOL. 21, NO. 20, MAR.
4-11, 1987./ The Guardian Building, Back Issues, 2700 19th St., SF, CA
94110, (415) 624-7560

3. INFORMATION ABOUT COMMON CAUSE./ 2030 M Street, N.W., Washington,
D.C., 20036

4. BAY AREA LAWYERS FOR THE ARTS, MULTIPLE PUBLICATIONS./ Fort Mason
Center, San Francisco, CA, (415) 775-7200

5.   CONFLICTS OF INTEREST AND CAMPAIGN CONTRIBUTIONS./California Fair
Political Practices Commission,(CFPPC), 428 J Street,(PO Box 807),
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 (916) 322-5662

6. POLITICAL REFORM ACT OF 1974 AS AMENDED-(CFPPC)

7. INFORMATION MANUAL FOR LOBBYING DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS OF THE
POLITICAL REFORM ACT. (CFPPC)

8. INFORMATION MANUAL ON CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS OF THE POLITICAL
REFORM ACT. (CFPPC)

9. 1986 RECORD KEEPING MANUAL, CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS OF THE
POLITICAL REFORM ACT OF 1974. (CFPPC)

10. A GUIDE FOR CANDIDATES AND TREASURERS. (CFPPC)

11. CAMPAIGN GUIDE FOR NONCONNECTED COMMITTEES./The Federal Election
Commission, (FEC),Washington, D.C. 20463, (202) 376-5140

12. FEDERAL ELECTION CAMPAIGN LAWS. (FEC)

13. CODE OF FEDERAL REGULATIONS, FEDERAL ELECTIONS, 11, REVISED JAN. 1,
1986./ The National Archives of the United States, Superintendent of
Documents, U.S. Government Printing Office, Wash., D.C., 20402, (202)
783-3238

Pueblo, Colorado, 81002, (303) 948-3334

15. FEDERAL DEPOSITORY LIBRARIES. (CIC)

16. YOUR RIGHT TO FEDERAL RECORDS. (CIC)

17. THE FIRST AMENDMENT HANDBOOK,/ Reporters Committee for Freedom of
the Press, 800 18th Street N.W., Suite 300, Wash. D.C., 20006, (202)
466-6313

2

18. THE FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT: WHAT IT IS AND HOW TO USE IT./
Freedom of Information Clearinghouse, PO Box 19367, Wash. D.C., 20036,
(202) 293-9142

19. FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT FILES KIT, and other publications./ Fund

20. OPEN MEETING LAWS./ Office of the Attorney General, Public Inquiries
Unit, PO Box 944255, Sacramento, CA 94244-2550, (916) 332-3360

21. RAISING HELL./ The Center For Investigative Reporting, 54 Mint
Street, San Francisco, CA, 94103, (415) 648-1000

22. CALIFORNIA FREEDOM OF INFORMATION COMMITTEE./ PO Box 900, Stockton,
CA 95201, (209) 943-6397

23. CENTER FOR PUBLIC INTEREST LAW, resource brochures./ c/o Public
Advocates, 1535 Mission Street, San Francisco, CA, 94103 (415) 431-7430

24. SAN FRANCISCANS FOR REASONABLE GROWTH, information/membership
brochure./ 870 Market Street, Suite 1117, San Francisco, CA, 94102,
(415) 392-6760

25. VOTER REGISTRATION MATERIALS/ INFORMATION FLYER ON INITIATIVE
PETITIONS./ Registrar of Voters, 155 City Hall, San Francisco, CA, 94102
(415) 554-4399

26. HOME AND BUSINESS COMPUTER ON-LINE INFORMATION ACCESS/ WESTERN UNION
EASYLINK SYSTEM/ INFOMASTER DATABASE SERVICE/ Western Union Telegraph
Co., One Lake Street S-5, Upper Saddle River, NJ, 07458, (800) 435-7375

27. Government, Political and Public Interest Publications List for
South End Press./ South End Press, Box 68, Astor Station, Boston, MA,
02123, (617) 266-0629

28. DIRECTORY OF SAN FRANCISCO CLUBS AND ORGANIZATIONS./ San Francisco
Chamber of Commerce, 465 California Street, San Francisco, CA, 94104,
(415) 392-4511

29. American Civil Liberties Union information brochure./ 1663 Mission
St., 4th Flr., San Francisco, CA, 94103 (415) 621-2488

30. Citizens Action League information brochure./ 2988 Mission Street,
San Francisco, CA, 94105, (415) 647-8450

31. San Francisco Planning & Urban Research Assn. information brochure./
312 Sutter Street, San Francisco, CA, 94108, (415) 781-8726

32. The State Bar of California information brochures./ 555 Franklin
St., San Francisco, CA, 94102, (415) 561-8200

33. Legal Aid Society information brochures./ 693 Mission Street, San
Francisco, CA, 94103, (415) 495-7374

35. American Arbitration Assn. information brochure./ 445 Bush, 5th
Flr., San Francisco, CA, 94108, (415) 981-3901

CPR
THE COUNCIL
FOR POLITICAL
RESPONSIVENESS

A Citizens Organization For Ethics And
Accountability In Government.

THE COUNCIL FOR POLITICAL RESPONSIVENESS
2261 Market Street
Suite 225
San Francisco, CA 94114-1693

SOME THOUGHTS...

A journey of a thousand miles begins with the first step.
—Old Turkish proverb

A government has something more to do than to govern, and levy taxes to pay the governors. It is something more than a police to arrest evil sick punish wrong. It must also encourage good, point out improvements, open roads of prosperity and diffuse life into all public enterprises. It should combine the best minds of the state for all the high ends for which society is established and to which man aspires. That gives us much to do.
—Joshua Chamberlain

I am only one, but still I am one. I cannot do everything, but still I can do something; and because I cannot do everything, I will not refuse to do the something that I can do.
—Edward Everett Horton

Ye shall know the truth and the truth shall make you free.
—John 8:32 (Engraved in marble at CIA headquarters entrance)

In a gentle way, you can shake the world. —Mahatma Gandhi

There are no great (people) in this world only great challenges which ordinary (people) rise to meet. —Admiral Bill Halsey

Perhaps the most valuable result of all education is the ability to make yourself do the thing you have to do, when it has to be done, whether you like it or not. This is the first lesson to be learned. —Thomas Henry Huxley

The rights of children are worth fighting for, because children are our best known source of adults. —Shari Lewis

...patriotism is not so much protecting the land of our fathers as preserving the land of our children. —Joel Osteen Gasset

Better light a candle than curse the darkness.
—Old Chinese proverb

Truth cannot be told so as to be understood without being believed. —William Blake

Ambition often puts men upon doing the meanest offices, so, climbing is performed in the same posture with creeping.
—Jonathan Swift

I believe that it is better to tell the truth than a lie. I believe it is better to be free than a slave. And I believe it is better to know than to be ignorant. —H. L. Mencken

Without rules there can be no game, without discipline there can be no freedom, and without responsibility there can be no rights. —Walter Hoving

This above all: to thine own self be true, And it must follow, as the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man.
—Shakespeare

HOW CAN YOU GET INVOLVED?

1. JOIN CPR:

NAME: _________________________________

PHONE: ________________________________

ADDRESS: ______________________________

CITY: __________________________________

STATE: __________ ZIP: ___________________

☐ $10.00 SPECIAL MEMBER
   ALL MEMBERSHIP CATEGORIES PLACE YOU ON OUR
   MAILING LIST FOR SPECIAL PROJECT INFORMATION
   AND CPR NEWS UPDATES.-

☐ $40.00 AUXILIARY MEMBER

☐ $150.00 SUPPORTING MEMBER

☐ $________ OPEN MEMBERSHIP

2. VOLUNTEER:

☐ I HAVE SPECIALIST SKILLS IN THE AREA OF __________

   AND WOULD LIKE TO VOLUNTEER MY TIME. __________

☐ I USUALLY HAVE FREE TIME THE FOLLOWING DAYS
   AND HOURS: _________________________________

   AND WOULD LIKE TO HELP WITH OFFICE AND ADMIN-
   ISTRATIVE AID TASKS

3. FIND 10 PEOPLE, WHOM YOU KNOW, WHO ARE NOT
   REGISTERED VOTERS AND ASK THEM TO REGISTER. FOL-
   LOW-UP WITH THEM AND HELP SEE THAT THEY DO.

4. INFORM YOURSELF, READ DIFFERENT VIEWPOINTS OF
   INFORMATION ON ISSUES THAT CONCERN YOU. MAKE
   SURE YOU HAVE ALL SIDES OF THE STORY.

5. WATCH CPR NEWS FOR NEW PROGRAMS AND PARTICI-
   PATE WHERE YOU CAN.

6. USE THE CPR CONTACT BOOK TO WRITE OR PHONE
   POLITICAL REPRESENTATIVES AND EXPRESS YOUR FEEL-
   INGS ON ISSUES WHICH THEY CAN HELP TO FINALIZE IN
   POSITIVE LEGISLATION.

SEND YOUR MEMBERSHIP FEE AND THE TOP HALF OF
THIS FORM TO:

THE COUNCIL FOR POLITICAL RESPONSIVENESS
2261 Market Street
Suite 225
San Francisco, CA 94114-1693

CPR is a non-partisan, non-profit community organization.
